title: README.md architecture diagram does not capture Pest Control dual role
evidence: Exact title match with ant-farm-zv46 ('README.md architecture diagram does not capture Pest Control dual role'). Both created on different dates (0jn7: 2026-02-21, zv46: 2026-02-22) but describe the same issue. The README.md architecture ASCII diagram (lines 43-61) shows Pest Control in a single box alongside Scout and Pantry without distinguishing its pre-spawn CCO role from its post-wave WWD/DMVDC role — the underlying issue is real and still present, but the bead is a duplicate of ant-farm-zv46.
priority_check: P3 is appropriate — the diagram is descriptive documentation; the missing distinction does not break functionality.
notes: Older of the two duplicates. The underlying issue (README diagram does not show Pest Control's dual-phase role) is STILL_VALID; only one bead should be retained.

title: fix: MEMORY.md references deleted _session-3be37d without noting its absence is expected
evidence: MEMORY.md is a user-maintained file at ~/.claude/projects/-Users-correy-projects-ant-farm/memory/MEMORY.md, outside the repo. The current MEMORY.md content (visible in session context) contains the 'Session Artifacts Are Retained' entry: 'The global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md was synced to match in session 3be37d after accidentally deleting a session directory.' The reference to _session-3be37d exists without any annotation explaining that the session directory itself is absent by design. The bead's requested fix (annotate with expected-absence note) has not been applied. NOTE: MEMORY.md is user-maintained and outside the repo — a fix here requires the user to manually edit their memory file, not a code commit.
priority_check: P3 is appropriate — this is a confusion risk for developers grepping session IDs, not a functional break.
notes: MEMORY.md is outside the repo and not editable via git commit. Any fix must be applied by the user directly to ~/.claude/projects/-Users-correy-projects-ant-farm/memory/MEMORY.md.

title: README.md deprecated agent row inconsistently formatted
evidence: Exact title match with ant-farm-c1n2 ('README.md deprecated agent row inconsistently formatted'). Both are open P3 bugs (4lcv: 2026-02-21, c1n2: 2026-02-22). The underlying issue is real: README.md line 309 shows the pantry-review row as '| `pantry-review` | Read, Write, Glob, Grep | ~~Review prompt composer: builds review briefs and combined previews~~ **DEPRECATED** — replaced by `fill-review-slots.sh` bash script; see RULES.md Step 3b |' — the strikethrough applies only to the description text, not the whole purpose column, while the DEPRECATED label is bold inline. This inconsistent formatting is still present.
priority_check: P3 is appropriate — cosmetic formatting inconsistency in a table row.
notes: Older of the two duplicates. The underlying issue is STILL_VALID; only one bead should be retained.

title: SETUP.md references wrong path for SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md
evidence: Exact title match with ant-farm-h41z ('SETUP.md references wrong path for SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md'). Both are open P3 bugs (9hxz: 2026-02-21, h41z: 2026-02-22). The underlying issue is real and still present: SETUP.md lines 61 and 121 both show 'cp orchestration/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md .' but the file actually lives at orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md (confirmed via glob — only one file exists at that path). Running the copy command as documented would fail with 'No such file or directory'.
priority_check: P3 may be under-prioritized — this is a broken copy command that would fail for new adopters following the setup guide. P2 could be argued.
notes: Older of the two duplicates. The wrong path appears in two places in SETUP.md (line 61 in Quick Setup Step 3, line 121 in Full Setup Step 1). Only one bead should be retained.

title: README.md Dirt Pushers mislabeled as review subagents
evidence: README.md line 41 reads: 'The system has three layers: the Queen (the orchestrator that never touches source code), Dirt Pushers (implementation and review subagents), and Pest Control (an independent verification agent that audits both).' The label 'implementation and review subagents' for Dirt Pushers is inaccurate. The architecture diagram on lines 43-61 shows a distinct fourth layer ('the Nitpickers (4 reviewers + Big Head)') for review work, which is separate from the Dirt Pusher layer. Dirt Pushers are implementation agents only. The mislabeling is still present.
priority_check: P3 is appropriate — documentation accuracy issue, not functional.

title: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE stale hardcoded values (model name, token budget, emoji)
evidence: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md line 8: '**Boss-Bot:** Claude Sonnet 4.5' — model name is stale (current model is Claude Sonnet 4.6; 'Boss-Bot' term also replaced by 'the Queen'). Line 324: 'Token budget used: ___K / 200K (___%)' — 200K token budget may be stale. Emoji throughout: lines 45-48 use 🔴 🟡 🟢 for conflict risk labels; lines 74, 91, 106 use 🟢 🟡 🟡 for execution option risk labels; lines 289, 307, 308 use ✅ / ❌ for checklist items. All three categories of stale values are still present.
priority_check: P3 is appropriate — this template is copied by users, not read by agents at runtime.
notes: Overlaps with ant-farm-19r3 ('fix: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md uses stale Boss-Bot term and Claude Sonnet 4.5 model') which focuses specifically on the Boss-Bot term and model name. ant-farm-pxsk is broader (includes token budget and emoji). The two beads are NOT exact duplicates — different titles and different scope. No dedup action needed.

title: SETUP.md duplicate/overlapping content between Quick Setup and Recipe Card
evidence: Exact title match with ant-farm-y719 ('SETUP.md duplicate/overlapping content between Quick Setup and Recipe Card'). Both are open P3 bugs (q3o6: 2026-02-21, y719: 2026-02-22). The underlying issue is real and still present: SETUP.md Quick Setup Step 2 (lines 38-55) shows a verbose markdown snippet for the Orchestration section of CLAUDE.md, and the Recipe Card section (lines 87-101) shows an almost identical but condensed version of the same snippet. Both instruct users to add the same content to their project CLAUDE.md, creating a maintenance surface where changes must be made in two places.
notes: Older of the two duplicates. The underlying issue is STILL_VALID; only one bead should be retained.

title: SETUP.md Prerequisites section duplicates Quick Setup commands
evidence: SETUP.md Prerequisites section (lines 9-14) shows a bash code block with './scripts/install-hooks.sh' and './scripts/sync-to-claude.sh'. SETUP.md Quick Setup Step 1 (lines 24-28) shows an identical bash code block with the same two commands. The duplication means any change to these commands requires updates in two places. The description in the bead was accurate: the fix (remove code block from Prerequisites, keep only in Quick Setup) has not been applied.
priority_check: P3 is appropriate — maintainability concern, not functional.
notes: Distinct from ant-farm-q3o6 / ant-farm-y719 which are about Quick Setup vs Recipe Card duplication. This bead is about Prerequisites vs Quick Setup duplication — a different pair of sections.

title: SETUP.md nested code fences break standard markdown rendering
evidence: SETUP.md Quick Setup Step 2 (lines 38-55) uses a 4-backtick outer fence ('````markdown') to contain an inner block with triple-backtick fences (lines 46, 49). In standard markdown — including GitHub Flavored Markdown — using N+1 backticks for the outer fence when the inner content contains N backticks is the correct approach for escaping nested code fences. The current file correctly applies this technique: the outer fence uses 4 backticks, the inner fences use 3. There are no cases in SETUP.md where an outer triple-backtick fence contains an inner triple-backtick fence (which would break rendering). The original issue has been resolved.
priority_check: P3 was appropriate when the issue was valid.
notes: If the original reporter saw the 4-backtick outer fence and still considers this broken in certain renderers (e.g., some editors that do not support 4-backtick outer fences), this verdict could be revised to NEEDS_CONTEXT. However, 4-backtick fencing is standard GFM behavior.

title: README.md architecture diagram does not capture Pest Control dual role
evidence: Exact title match with ant-farm-0jn7 ('README.md architecture diagram does not capture Pest Control dual role'). Newer of the pair (zv46: 2026-02-22, 0jn7: 2026-02-21). The underlying issue is real and still present: README.md ASCII diagram (lines 43-61) places Pest Control in the Scout/Pantry/Pest Control row showing CCO, WWD, DMVDC, and CCB functions, but does not distinguish that Pest Control runs at multiple distinct phases (pre-spawn as CCO, post-wave as WWD/DMVDC, post-review as CCB) — a reader cannot tell from the diagram alone that Pest Control is invoked three separate times.
notes: Newer of the two duplicates; ant-farm-0jn7 is the older canonical bead.
